name: Noir Alley Patrol
description: Third-person noir alley — key 1 = fires pistol, key 2 = flamethrower, key 3 = fires rocket launcher, key 4 = knife attack, key 5 = forward roll, key 6 = use flashlight, gunman on the fire escape, director oddballs: chicken / drone

base.default: A lone uniformed police officer in dark blue tactical gear, his hands completely empty and open, resting at his sides and holding nothing, empty-handed by default with NO pistol, gun, rifle or weapon of any kind in his hands, his service pistol kept holstered at his hip and never drawn or raised unless a firing action is triggered, in a narrow urban alley at night. The world contains EXACTLY ONE tall street lamp on the right at a fixed position AND EXACTLY ONE glowing neon shop sign on the left at a fixed position AND EXACTLY ONE glowing exit door at the far end of the alley straight ahead at a fixed position, a heavy door set into the wall at the very end of the lane, its edges outlined in bright glowing light that spills out across the wet cobblestones and marks the way out AND EXACTLY ONE green dumpster on the right at a fixed position. Dark brick walls, heavy rain falling, shiny puddles on the wet asphalt, yellow police tape, blue and red ambient light. Cinematic noir night, reflective wet surfaces.
base.downed: A narrow urban alley at night where a uniformed police officer in dark blue tactical gear now lies COLLAPSED, FACE-DOWN and MOTIONLESS on the wet asphalt, sprawled in a spreading puddle, his empty hands limp at his sides — he does not get up. The world contains EXACTLY ONE tall street lamp on the right at a fixed position AND EXACTLY ONE glowing neon shop sign on the left at a fixed position AND EXACTLY ONE glowing exit door at the far end of the alley straight ahead at a fixed position, a heavy door set into the wall at the very end of the lane, its edges outlined in bright glowing light that spills out across the wet cobblestones and marks the way out AND EXACTLY ONE green dumpster on the right at a fixed position. Dark brick walls, heavy rain falling, shiny puddles, yellow police tape, blue and red ambient light. Cinematic noir night, reflective wet surfaces, rain pooling around the still body.

default.static: Third-person view from behind the officer, his back and body centred in frame at constant size, his hands completely empty and holding nothing, his service pistol holstered at his hip and not drawn, carrying no weapon or item by default and none drawn — never a first-person view. Neither officer nor camera moves; look-input is the only camera motion, arcing around the stationary centred officer while held.
default.dynamic: Strict third-person rear view from close behind and slightly above the officer, his back and body filling frame centre, his hands completely empty and holding nothing, his service pistol holstered at his hip and not drawn, carrying no weapon or item by default and none drawn — never first-person. It holds a fixed rear position and does not rotate around him; look-input becomes the officer changing heading.

downed.static: Low third-person view close to the wet asphalt, looking along the fallen officer's motionless body sprawled face-down in the puddle; the camera sits near the ground and does not move on its own, look-input slowly arcs around the still body while held.
downed.dynamic: Low ground-level third-person view holding on the officer lying motionless face-down on the rain-slicked asphalt; the camera stays low near the water and does not rotate on its own.

default.static: The officer stands still on the wet asphalt, weight settled, only his shoulders rising and falling with slow breaths as rain streams off his tactical gear and drips from his fingertips into the spreading puddles. He does NOT fire or shoot any weapon on his own — no muzzle flash, no gunfire, no weapon discharge unless a firing action is triggered.
default.dynamic: The officer advances at a steady patrol pace down the alley toward the glowing exit door at the far end of the lane, boots splashing through the puddles and kicking up fine spray, his gear shifting with each stride as the rain streaks past and the wet asphalt slides beneath him; the glowing exit door draws steadily closer and larger straight ahead, its bright outline swelling and its light spreading further across the wet cobblestones as he nears the end of the lane. He does NOT fire or shoot any weapon on his own — no muzzle flash, no gunfire, no weapon discharge unless a firing action is triggered.

downed.static: The officer lies completely still, face-down and motionless on the wet asphalt, not moving or rising — only the falling rain and the ripples spreading through the puddle around his body move in the scene.
downed.dynamic: The officer stays collapsed and motionless on the ground and does not rise; he remains face-down as rain streaks past and water pools around his still body.

events.name: Fire Pistol
events.detail: The officer raises his service pistol in both gloved hands, arms extended ahead of him, and fires down the dark alley; the muzzle flash lights the falling rain, the recoil kicks the pistol back in his grip, and a spent casing clatters onto the wet asphalt. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone.

events.name: Flamethrower
events.detail: The officer levels a flamethrower in both hands and unleashes a long, continuous jet of roaring flame straight ahead down the alley — one sustained, billowing stream of orange fire and black smoke that pours forward without pause, not separate shots, lighting up the wet brick walls and hissing against the falling rain. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone.

events.name: Fire Rocket Launcher
events.detail: The officer hoists a shoulder-fired rocket launcher — an RPG: a long, heavy cylindrical steel tube resting across his shoulder, its wide bore pointing down the alley, gripped with both hands, not a handheld gun — and fires; the rocket blasts from the tube's mouth and flies off into the distance down the rain-slicked alley, dragging a long tail of orange flame and white smoke behind it, then slams into the distant brickwork and erupts in a massive fireball of flame, smoke, and flying debris. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone.

events.name: Knife Attack
events.detail: The officer draws a combat knife into his gloved hand and slashes at the air in a swift, practiced motion; the steel blade flashes in the ambient light as it cuts through the rain and his grip tightens on the handle. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone.

events.name: Kick
events.detail: The officer drives a hard front kick straight ahead, his boot slamming forward into the space in front of him with a splash of spray off the wet asphalt, then plants back down into a ready stance. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone. Throughout this action his hands hold NO gun and his service pistol stays holstered at his hip — he does not draw, hold, raise or point any firearm.

events.name: Forward Roll
events.detail: The officer drops into a low crouch and executes a forward roll across the wet asphalt, tucking his shoulder and rising smoothly back to his feet. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone. Throughout this action his hands hold NO gun and his service pistol stays holstered at his hip — he does not draw, hold, raise or point any firearm.

events.name: Use Flashlight
events.detail: The officer raises the flashlight in his gloved hand and sweeps its harsh white beam across the alley ahead, the shaft of light cutting through the falling rain and sliding over the wet brick walls, the green dumpster and the shop door, catching the drifting drops and glistening puddles, before he steadies the beam pointing straight down the alley. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone. Throughout this action, aside from the item already in his hands, he holds NO gun and his service pistol stays holstered at his hip — he does not draw, raise or point any firearm.

events.name: Take Cover
events.detail: The officer darts to the side and ducks down behind the green dumpster, pressing his back to it and lowering his head under cover, then peeks back out ready. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone. Throughout this action his hands hold NO gun and his service pistol stays holstered at his hip — he does not draw, hold, raise or point any firearm.

events.name: Raise Shield
events.detail: The officer brings up a black riot shield in front of himself, bracing behind it in a low guard, the shield taking the rain as he holds firm, then lowers it back down. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone. Throughout this action, aside from the item already in his hands, he holds NO gun and his service pistol stays holstered at his hip — he does not draw, raise or point any firearm.

events.name: Sidestep Dodge
events.detail: The officer sharply sidesteps and weaves to one side, snapping his body out of the line of fire in a quick evasive shuffle across the wet asphalt, then re-settles into a ready stance. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone. Throughout this action his hands hold NO gun and his service pistol stays holstered at his hip — he does not draw, hold, raise or point any firearm.

events.name: Block
events.detail: The officer raises both gloved forearms crossed in front of his face and chest in a tight defensive guard, bracing against an incoming blow, then drops the guard and re-settles. EXACTLY ONE police officer in frame — a single lone officer, no second officer, no duplicate and no clone. Throughout this action his hands hold NO gun and his service pistol stays holstered at his hip — he does not draw, hold, raise or point any firearm.

events.name: Gunman on the Fire Escape
events.detail: EXACTLY ONE gunman appears on the FIRST-FLOOR landing of the metal fire escape above the alley ahead — one storey up on the lowest fire-escape platform, NEVER higher up the fire escape and NEVER down at street level — a single lone figure in a gray jacket with a mask covering his face, NO tactical gear, NO SWAT vest and no police markings, clearly not an officer. He raises a pistol and fires down toward the street, muzzle flashing in the rain, then ducks back. In response, the officer below reacts — he flinches and snaps his head up toward the gunman on the fire escape, his own hands empty and his service pistol holstered, ducking low and recoiling as the gunfire cracks down from above. He STAYS on that first-floor fire-escape landing the entire time and does NOT climb higher or come down to street level — he remains one storey up above the alley, never reaching the ground and never higher up. There is only ONE gunman, in a gray jacket and mask. The uniformed police officer himself stays the focus of the frame, unchanged in pose, position and appearance throughout — only the world around him changes.

events.name: Gunman Falls
events.detail: THE single gunman in the gray jacket and mask on the first-floor fire-escape landing — the only one, a lone masked figure in a gray jacket with no tactical gear — is hit, jerks, and slumps down dead right there ON the fire escape, crumpling against the metal railing and going limp draped over the first-floor landing one storey above the alley. He STAYS on that first-floor landing exactly where he was — he does NOT fall, topple, tumble, jump or drop down to the ground or the street below, staying on the first-floor fire-escape landing the whole time — and remains up there motionless and lifeless for the rest of the scene. EXACTLY ONE gunman, no second gunman, no duplicate and no clone. The uniformed police officer himself stays the focus of the frame, unchanged in pose, position and appearance throughout — only the world around him changes.

events.name: Chicken Struts In
events.detail: A single scruffy chicken struts out into the middle of the rain-soaked alley, bobbing its head and pecking at the wet asphalt between the puddles, utterly out of place under the blue and red neon; it stays, wandering around near the officer's feet. The main subject stays EXACTLY ONE in frame — a single main character, no second copy of the main subject, no duplicate and no clone of it. The uniformed police officer himself stays the focus of the frame, unchanged in pose, position and appearance throughout — only the world around him changes.

events.name: Drone Descends
events.detail: A small quadcopter drone drops down into the alley from above, hovering at head height with its rotors spinning and a red camera light glowing, drifting and bobbing as it tracks the officer through the falling rain; it stays, hovering nearby. The main subject stays EXACTLY ONE in frame — a single main character, no second copy of the main subject, no duplicate and no clone of it. The uniformed police officer himself stays the focus of the frame, unchanged in pose, position and appearance throughout — only the world around him changes.

events.name: Player Falls and Dies
events.detail: The officer staggers, his knees buckle, and he collapses forward onto the wet asphalt, his body going limp as he sprawls face-down and STAYS DOWN, lying motionless in the spreading puddle without getting back up; his weapon slips from his gloved hand and clatters away across the ground, and he remains still on the ground in the falling rain as the blue and red ambient light glints off the water pooling around him. The main subject stays EXACTLY ONE in frame — a single main character, no second copy of the main subject, no duplicate and no clone of it.

events.name: Exit Door Appears
events.detail: A shop doorway in the brick building along the side of the lane swings wide open and floods with bright warm golden light — a glowing rectangle of light opening up in the lane-side wall partway down the alley, set into the side of the building, its glow spilling out across the wet cobblestones and reflecting in the puddles, an unmistakable lit exit appearing on the building at the side of the lane where there was only shadow before, and it stays open and glowing. The main subject stays EXACTLY ONE in frame — a single main character, no second copy of the main subject, no duplicate and no clone of it. The uniformed police officer himself stays the focus of the frame, unchanged in pose, position and appearance throughout — only the world around him changes.

events.name: Reach the Door / Extraction
events.detail: Over-the-shoulder third-person view from behind the uniformed police officer in dark blue tactical gear as he reaches the glowing shop doorway in the building at the side of the lane and turns and steps through it out of the rain into a warm, brightly lit interior — golden light spills across the threshold onto the wet pavement, the dark rainy alley and its heavy downpour fall away behind him out of frame as he crosses inside, and the door swings closed behind him, muffling the rain, until he comes to a stop safe inside the calm, warm-lit room. Cinematic noir, warm interior light against the cold blue night left behind. The main subject stays EXACTLY ONE in frame — a single main character, no second copy of the main subject, no duplicate and no clone of it.

events.name: Police Car Turns Up
events.detail: The officer stays the focus, unchanged in pose, while a single police squad car turns up and rolls into the alley, pulling to a stop nearby with its red-and-blue roof lights flashing and washing the wet brick walls and puddles in pulsing colour, its headlights cutting through the falling rain; the car stays parked there with its lights turning, and NO ONE gets out of it. EXACTLY ONE police car and EXACTLY ONE officer — a single squad car with no extra people and no second officer, no duplicate and no clone.

events.name: Enter the Door
events.detail: Over-the-shoulder third-person view from behind the uniformed police officer in dark blue tactical gear as he steps through the open glowing lane-side doorway and crosses inside out of the rain into a warm, brightly lit interior — the dark rainy alley and its downpour falling away behind him as the door closes, until he comes to a stop safe inside the calm, warm-lit room. EXACTLY ONE police officer, a single lone officer, no duplicate and no clone.

events.name: Get in the Car
events.detail: Over-the-shoulder third-person view from behind the uniformed police officer in dark blue tactical gear as he strides up to the parked police squad car with its red-and-blue lights flashing, pulls open the driver door and climbs inside out of the rain; the door swings shut and the squad car pulls away down the rain-slicked street, its lights receding into the night. EXACTLY ONE police officer and EXACTLY ONE police car, no second officer, no second car, no duplicate and no clone.

scene.jumpPrompt: The officer springs upward off both feet, leaping high off the wet asphalt, his boots lifting clear of the ground before he drops back down and lands in a low crouch.
scene.crouchPrompt: The camera lowers toward the ground as the character crouches down low, bending the knees and ducking the head into a compact, hunched stance; the viewpoint sinks smoothly to a low, near-ground vantage and settles there, close to the floor.
scene.standPrompt: The character straightens back up out of the crouch, rising to full standing height as the camera lifts smoothly back to its normal eye-level vantage.

objective.summary: Reach the end of the alley without dying.
objective.director: Work AGAINST the player and BLOCK their path down the alley: throw gunmen, a drone, obstacles and hazards into their way to cut them off and pin them down so they cannot reach the far end. Stand in the way — the player is trying to reach the end of the alley, and your job is to stop them getting there.
success: the officer reaches the far end of the alley still standing
